evidence: `useIsMobile` intentionally folds tablet/coarse-pointer detection into "mobile" (`src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx`), while downstream consumers treat it as phone-only behavior (for example mobile tap/double-tap interaction flow in `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMobileInteractions.ts`), creating ambiguous control-flow expectations.

Timestamp update logic is split across `src/shared/hooks/useUpdatingTimestamp.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/useProcessingTimestamp.ts` with near-duplicate parse/refresh behavior, increasing drift risk for edge cases and update cadence changes.

Cross-cutting orchestration payload builders (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`) contain long conditional assembly paths that are hard to reason about uniformly.

Event vocabulary in `src/shared/lib/typedEvents.ts` mixes naming styles (`openSettings`, `generation-star-updated`, `timeline:pending-add`, `realtime:auth-heal`), making discovery and grep-based maintenance harder.

Several API surfaces expose generic string labels without bounded domain naming, e.g. `openSettings.tab?: string` in `src/shared/lib/typedEvents.ts` and `tool_type?: string` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts` despite having canonical constants in `src/shared/lib/toolConstants.ts`.
Terminology around task linkage varies (`taskId`, `primaryTaskId`, `tasks` array) across `src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts`, which weakens intent signaling.

Browser timeout refs are typed as `NodeJS.Timeout` in frontend hooks (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMobileInteractions.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts`), coupling DOM code to Node typings and risking environment/type-lib incompatibility.

Unsafe casting from Supabase JSON to strong runtime assumptions appears in `src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts` (`data?.tasks as string[] | null`) and `src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts` (`row.params as Record<string, unknown>`), with no runtime narrowing for element/value shape.

Task payload builders rely heavily on `Record<string, unknown>` outputs (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts`), reducing compile-time guarantees for API contracts.

findings.title: DOM timer handles are typed with NodeJS.Timeout in browser-only hooks
findings.evidence: Both `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMobileInteractions.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts` declare timeout refs as `NodeJS.Timeout | null`, but these hooks run in browser contexts where `setTimeout` returns `number`/`ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>` depending on lib setup.
findings.suggestion: Switch timeout ref types to `ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>` so browser and Node lib combinations remain type-correct without environment-specific assumptions.

findings.title: Cross-module contracts remain stringly typed despite canonical constants
findings.evidence: `src/shared/lib/toolConstants.ts` defines a canonical tool ID set, but `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts` still exposes `tool_type?: string`; similarly `src/shared/lib/typedEvents.ts` uses unconstrained string payload fields like `openSettings.tab?: string` and `realtime:auth-heal.priority: string`.
findings.suggestion: Replace unconstrained string contracts with narrow unions or exported constant-backed types, then thread those types through emitters and task payload builders.

findings.title: Mobile classification semantics are ambiguous between hooks and consumers
findings.evidence: `src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx` computes `isMobile` using width, coarse pointer, and tablet heuristics, while consumers like `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMobileInteractions.ts` apply mobile-only interaction logic based on that boolean, blurring phone-vs-tablet intent and behavior.
findings.suggestion: Split phone/tablet/coarse-pointer semantics into explicit booleans and migrate interaction hooks to consume intent-specific flags instead of the overloaded `isMobile` value.
